---
title: "Module 5 — Approximate Inference & Bayesian Classification"
format:
  html:
    theme: cosmo
    toc: false
---

<!-- Thin module landing (repo convention: index.qmd is a landing, never a
     lecture deck). Decks live beside it; see CLAUDE.md. -->

Binarize the outcome — hypertensive or not — and Module 4's conjugacy breaks:
no closed-form posterior survives (**Project 2 opens here**). Logistic
regression models $p(y{=}1\mid\mathbf{x}) = \sigma(\mathbf{w}^{\!\top}\mathbf{x})$;
Newton–Raphson finds $\hat{\mathbf{w}}$ in a handful of steps; then approximation
becomes the craft — a Gaussian at the MAP (Laplace) or draws from the
unnormalized posterior (Metropolis).

| Deck | Link |
|------|------|
| Lecture A — Breaking Conjugacy: Logistic Regression & Newton's Method | [m5a-logistic-newton](m5a-logistic-newton.html) |
| Lecture B — Laplace Approximation & a Taste of Sampling | [m5b-laplace-sampling](m5b-laplace-sampling.html) |

Companion activity: [week04 logistic-regression / Newton explorer](https://gchism94.github.io/info521-activities-2026/week04-logistic-regression/)
(embedded live in Lecture A — the slug is week-named; modules ≠ weeks).

[← All slide decks](../../index.html)
